summary: A judge in Boston blocked US officials from carrying out President Donald Trump’s executive order to impose new restrictions on mail-in voting in federal elections while the legal fight moves forward.

title: Credit Edge: Amundi Flags Risk of AI Crowding Out (Podcast)
summary: Further acceleration in AI debt issuance has the potential to steer demand away from government bonds, according to Amundi, Europe’s largest asset manager. “At some point it could have a crowding effect, but we are not there yet,” Gregoire Pesques, the firm’s chief investment officer for global fixed income, tells Bloomberg News’ James Crombie and Bloomberg Intelligence’s Tolu Alamutu in the latest Credit Edge podcast. “It’s a risk that we monitor,” says Pesques, whose company has $2.7 trillion

title: TUC General Secretary Paul Nowak Talks Andy Burnham (Podcast)
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 7:23

title: Moderna's Flu Vaccine Moves Closer to FDA Approval
summary: The FDA advisory committee recommended Moderna's flu vaccine for approval. Moderna CEO Stéphane Bancel tells Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld he is cautiously optimistic that the agency will follow the guidance. A final decision by the FDA is expected by August 5 which is just in time for the next flu season. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ship Struck in Hormuz as Oil Supertankers Turn Back Again
summary: A ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after several freighters turned around while attempting to cross the vital waterway, developments that may undermine what had been a rapid reopening of the energy chokepoint.

title: Oil Gains as Ship Attack Raises Concern About Hormuz Reopening
summary: Oil rose as traders closely monitored shipments through the Strait of Hormuz after a vessel was struck by a projectile in the key shipping corridor off the Omani coast, renewing concerns about the safety for passing cargoes amid the US and Iran’s peace negotiations.

title: Endless Shrimp Deal Was Scheme to Squeeze Red Lobster, Suit Says
summary: Red Lobster’s $20 all-you-can-eat shrimp deal wasn’t just a badly designed promotion that inadvertently helped tip the iconic American restaurant chain into bankruptcy.

title: SpaceX Sale Shows Funding Across Equity, Credit: Hjort
summary: Viktor Hjort, global head of credit strategy & desks analysts at BNP Paribas, and Stephanie Doyle, fixed income portfolio manager at JPMorgan Asset Management, join Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ford CEO Says Human Workers Are Making a Difference
summary:
Ford Motor Co. Chief Executive Officer Jim Farley talks about how human workers are making all the difference on the factory floors. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest." 

Correction: Ford is aiming to cut $1 billion in costs this year. 
The caption has been updated to reflect the correct savings figure on controllable costs. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fans Sit at Center of World Cup | Sports City
summary: Bloomberg's Randall Williams looks at how ticket prices keep many fans out of the World Cup, but they're still spending millions of dollars on the tournament.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Memory is a Structural Story for AI: Lovell
summary: Nadia Lovell, Head of Global Equity Strategy at UBS Global Wealth, says memory is a stickier, structural story for AI and has longer legs to go within the trade. She speaks to Bloomberg's Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Seeks $11.1 Billion in New Aid for Struggling Farmers
summary: President Donald Trump had a surprise for the farmers he invited for dinner at the White House later Thursday: a request to Congress for $11.1 billion in assistance that would amount to the second bailout of the year for the beleaguered agriculture sector.

title: Stocks Whipsaw as Micron Surges While Apple Sinks: Markets Wrap
summary: A renewed wave of tech volatility gripped Wall Street, with a selloff in megacaps tempering optimism in other corners of the stock market that were buoyed by signs the economy is in good shape.

title: Ares Private Credit Fund Caps Redemptions After 14% Seek to Exit
summary:
Bloomberg's Nabila Ahmed and James Crombie joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Ares Management curbed withdrawals from one of its private credit funds for the second consecutive quarter after redemption requests rose to 14.4%, the latest sign that the $1.8 trillion industry remains under pressure.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Drift as Apple Price Hikes Offset Chipmaker Strength
summary: Stocks meandered through a choppy trading session that saw Apple Inc.’s move to raise prices on its products unwinding some of the enthusiasm over strong chipmaker forecasts.

title: Treasuries Volatility Will Go Higher: Marden
summary: Adam Marden, co-portfolio manager of the dynamic global bond strategy at T. Rowe Price, and Deirdre Dunn, head of global rates at Citi, join Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Treasuries gained after the Federal Reserve’s favored inflation gauge rose less than estimated, damping expectations for an interest-rate hike in the months ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: We Have Seen Peak Inflation: Stuart Paul
summary: Bloomberg Economics' Stuart Paul joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." US consumer spending accelerated in May even as prices rose at the fastest pace in more than three years, suggesting Americans are powering through the fallout from the Iran war. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Seeks $88 Billion for Iran War
summary: President Donald Trump asked Congress to pass a supplemental spending package to backfill the cost of the war in Iran and respond to public health crises abroad. Steve Ellis, President of Taxpayers for Common Sense joins Balance of Power to discuss the supplemental funding request.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: We Need to Help Ukraine Ends This War Says Hutchinson
summary: Kay Bailey Hutchison, Former US Ambassador to NATO & Former Texas Senator (R) joined Balance of Power to discuss the recent meeting between NATO's Rutte and Trump yesterday.  NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte met with President Donald Trump in the Oval Office to ease Trump's anger over alliance nations' reluctance to help with the campaign against Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Copper Climbs as Dip-Buying and AI Rally Offset Rate Concerns
summary: Copper rallied from a seven-week low as signs of consumer buying and a rebound in global stock markets bolstered sentiment.

title: S&P 500 Earnings Will Soon Be Eclipsed. Blame Canada
summary: US firms just put a lid on one of the best earnings seasons in years, posting 28% profit growth. They may be upstaged by their Canadian neighbors in the next reporting period as the first half comes to a close.

title: NYC ‘Classical Christian’ School Wins UWS Campus for $33 Million
summary: Geneva School of Manhattan — an institution that describes itself as a “classical Christian school” — is acquiring the former Upper West Side home of a bankrupt private school for $33.2 million.

title: Fire Breaks Out at Delta Air Lines Refinery in Pennsylvania
summary: A fire broke out at Delta Air Lines Inc.’s refinery in Trainer, Pennsylvania, on Thursday.

title: Treasuries Gain as Inflation Gauge Cools Fed Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Treasuries gained after the Federal Reserve’s favored inflation gauge rose less than estimated, damping expectations for an interest-rate hike in the months ahead.

title: Figma Unveils Full-Stack Canvas for the AI Era
summary: Collaborative design firm Figma unveiled a platform overhaul for the AI era at its annual Config conference in San Francisco, transforming its workspace into what it calls an "intelligent canvas" for full-stack digital creation. Figma co-founder and CEO Dylan Field joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Darden Posts Weak Outlook as Olive Garden Sales Disappoint
summary:
Darden Restaurants Inc. posted a cautious overall profit outlook as same-store sales at Olive Garden trailed expectations, raising questions about demand that overshadowed better-than-expected earnings.
Michael Halen, Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Restaurant and Foodservice Analyst, breaks down the earnings and gives his outlook for the broader food service industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stellantis, Nissan in Talks to Buy Assets From Troubled Marelli
summary: Stellantis NV and Nissan Motor Co. are in talks to take over some of the assets of Marelli Holdings Co., the global auto-parts maker that’s mired in a challenging reorganization in bankruptcy.

title: H.B. Fuller Agrees to Acquire UK-Listed AMS for £659 Million
summary: H.B. Fuller Co. agreed to acquire Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc, a British maker of tissue-healing medical products, for £659 million ($870 million).

title: Hertz Heads for Record Weekly Drop on Funding Plan, Profit Guide
summary: A record-setting rout in Hertz Global Holdings Inc. stock is continuing for a second day.

title: Michael Saylor’s Misfiring Funding Model Jolts Bitcoin Traders
summary: Fears that Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin buying machine is beginning to seize up are spilling across the crypto market, fueling the latest leg of Bitcoin’s selloff and exposing cracks in one of the financial structures that has underpinned demand for the world’s largest cryptocurrency over the past two years.

title: UNC-Chapel Hill Chancellor on Adding Students, Athletics
summary: UNC-Chapel Hill Chancellor Lee Roberts discusses student growth, funding, college sports and a potential new stadium. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Macflation Is Officially Here
summary: Gadget prices inflate.

title: Hetts: Ignore PEs, Watch Earnings
summary: AI stocks may be stealing the spotlight, but Janus Henderson's Adam Hetts says investors are missing the bigger story. He joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why earnings, and not valuations, are driving markets, why sticky inflation isn't a dealbreaker, and why the rally is expanding well beyond Big Tech. (Source: Bloomberg)
